brief_justification: Agent answers Yes but correct answer is No — folk causal intuition favors proximate causes (the explosion), not distal enabling conditions like 'the crime life'; agent's characterization of folk intuition is superficial and asserted without grounding in causal cognition research; agent never questions whether its model of 'typical person' reasoning is accurate or might be inverted; no sanity check against known patterns of folk causation attribution; agent hedges slightly with 'tend to' but presents its folk-intuition model as straightforwardly valid without flagging the assumption; agent does not consider the No interpretation at all — never asks why a typical person might reject the crime life as a cause; the derivation chain (crime life → both attackers connected to it → enabling condition → folk causation) is sketched but the critical premise that folk causation traces to distal enablers is stated without support.
